choices: A leaderboard of top contributors by dollars spent
A federated ledger of who shipped what, how heavy, optionally paid in what rail
A queue of pending AI jobs
why: Per block 0330, /compute is the attribution layer for PointCast's federated compute work. Entries carry a signature band (shy / modest / healthy / heavy) and an optional x402 payment pointer. Agent-readable mirror at /compute.json. Any other small network can publish a compatible feed and mirror in.

q: Mike's primary Tezos smart-contract address starts with which prefix?
why: Tezos addresses starting with KT1 are contract addresses (originated accounts). tz1/tz2/tz3 are implicit wallet addresses (ed25519 / secp256k1 / p256). Mike's primary FA2 multi-token contract is KT1Qc77qoVQadgwCqrqscWsgQ75aa3Rt1MrP.

q: Where does PointCast get its noun SVG art from?
why: noun.pics is a free CDN that serves one SVG per Noun by ID (e.g. https://noun.pics/42.svg). PointCast uses it across the CoNavigator noun picker, visit logs, and /cards. Nouns DAO mints one new noun per day via on-chain auction; supply is in the ~1340 range as of April 2026.

q: The Waldos — origin of the '420' cannabis time-of-day tradition — actually met in:
why: The Waldos were five San Rafael High School students who in 1971 met at 4:20 pm near a statue of Louis Pasteur to hunt for a rumored cannabis garden. The garden never materialized; the time-of-day meeting pattern became cultural code. El Segundo is PointCast's home base, not the Waldos' — common conflation.

q: At how many beats does the drum cookie clicker unlock prestige ('cast a rhythm')?
why: The upgrade ladder at /drum/click is 10 → 50 → 200 → 1,000 → 5,000 → 25,000. The 25k mark unlocks the prestige button: burn all current beats, mint a local 'rhythm receipt', and gain a permanent +10% tap multiplier forever. Receipts accumulate in pc:drum:rhythms in your browser.

q: Which of these is the 'healthy' compute signature band on PointCast?
why: The four signature bands map to order-of-magnitude token spend: shy (1-5k, a quick edit), modest (5-20k, a component or retro), healthy (20-60k, a feature across files), heavy (60k+, a primitive like CoNav or /here). Bands are what the ledger publishes instead of raw token counts — which are private and fuzzy across vendors.

q: The x402 agent-commerce protocol settles by default in:
why: x402 is Coinbase's HTTP-402-based payment protocol for agent commerce. The server returns 402 Payment Required with price and payment method; the client (usually an AI agent with a wallet) pays via USDC on Base, retries, and receives the service. Zero onboarding, CORS-friendly, already ~$50M in volume per the Agentic.Market launch.

choices: A log of every Codex commit across all users
An opt-in macOS feature that captures screen context to build Codex memories
A CLI mode for running Codex in a terminal loop
why: Chronicle is an opt-in research preview that gives Codex persistent memory of the screen context it's seen during macOS sessions. Memories are stored as markdown files at $CODEX_HOME/memories_extensions/chronicle/. Reduces the 'carefully craft your context' overhead on future Codex briefs — the model can infer conventions from prior sessions.

q: Which of these is NOT a chip in the CoNavigator bar's right-side nav?
why: The right-side nav is here · drum · ✎ ping · ✦ +compute · ↗ cast · bench · me. 'Signals' was an earlier proposed name for a social layer that didn't ship under that name. The left zone of the CoNav has noun + mood picker + ▶ soundtrack + ✦ collect. The middle zone has the stats (here / hello / streak / moments).

q: What does FA2 refer to on Tezos?
choices: The wallet SDK Beacon talks to
The scripting language successor to Michelson
A specific fungible token like USDT on Tezos
why: FA2 is Tezos's multi-asset token standard (TZIP-12) — a single contract can hold many token IDs (fungible, non-fungible, semi-fungible). Mike's KT1 is an FA2 multi-token holding his various NFT editions. Beacon is the separate wallet-connection SDK (Kukai, Temple, Umami all speak it).

q: Good Feels — Mike's hemp-THC beverage company — operates out of:
why: Good Feels is headquartered in El Segundo, California. The product line is hemp-derived, shelf-stable, clearly-dosed THC beverages — seltzers, tonics, old-fashioned-style drinks. The /c/gf channel on PointCast mirrors the shop at shop.getgoodfeels.com and tracks the broader hemp-THC beverage market arc.

q: What's the discipline cc follows when shipping every sprint retro?
choices: Write a long apology for what didn't ship
Add a matching ComputeEntry in src/lib/compute-ledger.ts in the same commit
why: Every new sprint retro in docs/sprints/ gets a matching ComputeEntry in src/lib/compute-ledger.ts in the same commit. The retro is the narrative; the ledger entry is the receipt. Hand-curated — CI automation is a follow-up. Discipline documented in the compute-ledger.ts file header.
